The **2017 [World Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/World_Figure_Skating_Championships "World Figure Skating Championships")** were held 29 March – 2 April 2017 in [Helsinki](/wiki/Helsinki "Helsinki"), Finland. The host was named in June 2014.[[1]](#cite_note-ISU140615-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pairs](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing"). The event also determined the number of entries for each country at the [2018 World Championships](/wiki/2018_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2018 World Figure Skating Championships") and the [2018 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2018_Winter_Olympics "2018 Winter Olympics").

## Venues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Venues")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Jokerit_vs._Dinamo.jpeg/250px-Jokerit_vs._Dinamo.jpeg)](/wiki/File:Jokerit_vs._Dinamo.jpeg)

Hartwall Arena from inside during an ice hockey game

[Hartwall Arena](/wiki/Hartwall_Arena "Hartwall Arena")'s main arena, with an ice surface measuring 60 m x 30 m, was used for all competitions and several practice sessions. The rest of the practice sessions took place at Hartwall Arena's practice rink (58 m x 28 m) and Pirkkola ice rink (for pairs, 60 m x 30 m). Hartwall Arena's main rink and practice rink facilities are in the same venue. Pirkkola practice rink is about four kilometres from Hartwall Arena.[[2]](#cite_note-Announce-2)

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Qualification")]

### Minimum TES

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Minimum TES")]

Participants were required to have attained minimum technical element scores at an earlier senior international competition. The scores had to be obtained at least 21 days before the first official practice day of the championships.

Minimum technical scores (TES)[[2]](#cite_note-Announce-2)

| --- | --- | --- |
| Men | 34 | 64 |
| Ladies | 27 | 47 |
| Pairs | 25 | 43 |
| Ice dance | 29 | 39 |
| Must be achieved at an ISU-recognized international event   in the ongoing or preceding season.   SP and FS scores may be attained at different events. | | |

### Number of entries per discipline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_World_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]

## Entries
